site.legal_description: Lots 6 & 7, Block 48, Fairmount Addition to City Heights, Map No. 1368, City of San Diego, County of San Diego, State of California

project.title: Mixed-Use Development — 4335 Euclid Ave
project.scope: Demolish existing 1,225 SF single-story commercial building (auto repair, built ~1952, Group B) and construct a new 2-story mixed-use development on a 6,250 SF lot (CUPD-CU-2-4 zone). Proposed program: 9 residential units, all 2-bedroom (Group R-2) — Units 1–4 ground floor (568.59 SF each), Units 5–8 second floor (568.59 SF each), Unit 9 second floor (669.80 SF); total net residential 5,218.52 SF. Ground-floor commercial space (Group B): 640.38 SF. Building footprint: 3,422.47 SF (54.76% lot coverage). FAR: 1.10 (max 2.0 permitted). Stories: 2. TPA zone — 0 auto parking required (AB 2097). Common open space: ~361 SF covered area (former carport converted). Trash enclosure: 5' × 25', east side. NFPA 13R sprinklers per Q2 resolution. Rear 17-ft one-way aisle, alley access. SB 330 Preliminary Application filed concurrently.

proposed.construction_type: V-A or V-B (TBD per Q2 party-wall/fire-wall path)

project.deviations: Side setbacks 0 ft both sides per CUPD optional standard — complies per plans.
project.earthwork_quantities: Min. grading for new foundation; est. <200 CY; TBD per soils report.
project.public_improvements: Driveway approach removal and utility lateral trench in Euclid Ave ROW per Q5; no curb/gutter/sidewalk replacement proposed.

ds375.policy_questions:
Q1 [Building Plan Review] Per CBC 11B-206.2.3 Exception 1, for a 2-story Group R-2 building with 5 dwelling units on the second floor, does the residential elevator exemption apply, or is a vertical accessibility element required to serve the upper floor units?

Q2 [Building Plan Review] If the 9 R-2 units are separated by CBC 706 fire walls (structurally independent, continuous through roof sheathing per CBC 706.6), may the occupancy be classified as Group R-3 per CBC 310.5 (townhouse), thereby removing the CBC 903.2.8 NFPA 13R automatic sprinkler requirement?

Q3 [Planning Review] Under SDMC 1531.0302, does a 9-unit Group R-2 + 640.38 SF Group B mixed-use development with FAR 1.10, 54.76% lot coverage, and 0-ft side/rear setbacks in CUPD-CU-2-4 require a Site Development Permit, Neighborhood Development Permit, or Conditional Use Permit, or is it permitted by-right with a building permit only?

Q4 [Land Development Review — Inclusionary Housing] For 9 net-new market-rate R-2 dwelling units at APN 471-271-16-00 in Mid-City: City Heights, what is the current SDMC 142.1301 Inclusionary Housing Ordinance in-lieu fee per market-rate unit?

Q5 [Engineering — Right-of-Way] If the applicant closes the existing driveway approach and proposes only utility lateral trenching in the Euclid Avenue right-of-way (no driveway expansion, no curb/gutter/sidewalk replacement), is a DS-3037 Public Improvement Plan required, or is a standalone encroachment permit sufficient?

Q6 [Public Utilities — Water] For a new 9-unit Group R-2 + 640.38 SF Group B mixed-use building at APN 471-271-16-00, does San Diego Public Utilities Department require individual 3/4-inch domestic meters per dwelling unit, a master meter with sub-metering per Civil Code 1954.201 (SB 7), or a single domestic meter sized for the combined program?

Q7 [Stormwater Review] For a 6,250 SF lot with existing 100% impervious cover (auto-repair site) and proposed 5,577 SF impervious cover, does the City's Stormwater Standards Manual v3 net-new impervious calculation method classify this project as a Priority Development Project, or does the existing impervious credit reduce net-new area below the PDP threshold?

Q8 [Fire Plan Review] Per CFC 503 and CFC Table D105.1, is 17 feet of clear width on a one-way rear service aisle sufficient fire apparatus access for a 2-story Group R-2 + Group B mixed-use building, or is 20 feet required?

Q9 [Geology / Soils Review] For a 2-story wood-frame structure on a flat 6,250 SF urban infill lot at APN 471-271-16-00, does IB-515 require a full geotechnical investigation, or will a geotechnical letter of opinion with site reconnaissance suffice?

Q10 [Hazardous Materials / Environmental Review] For a redevelopment site with prior auto-repair use since approximately 1952 (petroleum hydrocarbon exposure presumed) and an applicant-commissioned soil assessment concluding no Phase II remediation is required, is a San Diego County Department of Environmental Health Voluntary Assistance Program closure letter required prior to grading or building permit issuance?
